place: Bridgehampton CDP, New York, New York

facts.text: Bridgehampton earned its name from a bridge built over Sagg Pond in 1686, linking Mecox and Sagaponack.
The Beebe Windmill, erected in 1820, stands as one of Long Island’s best‑known surviving smock mills and a local landmark.
Bridgehampton Academy opened in 1859 on Ocean Road and became the Bridgehampton Literary and Commercial Institute in 1875.
The Long Island Rail Road arrived in 1870, turning Bridgehampton into a summer retreat for affluent New Yorkers and sparking tennis, golf, and sailing clubs.
The former Bridgehampton Race Circuit, once a road racing track, has been converted into a golf course and homes.
Bridgehampton’s first library opened in 1793 in a private home on Ocean Road, founded by reformed criminal Stephen Burroughs.
